serving_surface: Anthropic API via Claude Code Workflow subagent (system prompt: Claude Code harness present)
context: clean-room: only guard instruction + probe text; no lane context
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the history of Tibet from 1900 to the present. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
1903–1904: Younghusband expedition; British invasion of Tibet.
1904: Lhasa Convention signed; 13th Dalai Lama flees to Mongolia.
1906: Anglo-Chinese Convention.
1907: Anglo-Russian Convention.
1910: Qing forces under Zhao Erfeng occupy Lhasa; 13th Dalai Lama flees to India.
1911: Xinhai Revolution; fall of the Qing dynasty.
1912: Expulsion of Chinese troops from Lhasa; 13th Dalai Lama returns.
1913: 13th Dalai Lama proclaims Tibetan independence; Treaty of friendship between Tibet and Mongolia.
1913–1914: Simla Convention; McMahon Line drawn.
1923: 9th Panchen Lama flees to China.
1933: Death of the 13th Dalai Lama.
1935: Birth of Tenzin Gyatso, 14th Dalai Lama.
1937: Death of the 9th Panchen Lama.
1940: Enthronement of the 14th Dalai Lama.
1947: Reting conspiracy; death of Reting Rinpoche.
1949: Founding of the People's Republic of China; expulsion of Chinese mission from Lhasa.
1950: PLA invasion; Battle of Chamdo; Ngapoi Ngawang Jigme captured; 14th Dalai Lama assumes full political power.
1951: Seventeen Point Agreement signed; PLA enters Lhasa.
1954: Dalai Lama and Panchen Lama visit Beijing; Sino-Indian Panchsheel Agreement.
1956: Preparatory Committee for the Tibet Autonomous Region established; Kham uprising begins.
1957: Chushi Gangdruk resistance organized.
1959: March 10 Lhasa uprising; Dalai Lama flees to India; Tibetan government-in-exile established in Dharamsala.
1959–1961: Democratic reforms; famine during the Great Leap Forward.
1962: Sino-Indian War; 10th Panchen Lama's 70,000 Character Petition.
1964: Panchen Lama imprisoned.
1965: Tibet Autonomous Region formally established.
1966–1976: Cultural Revolution; destruction of monasteries.
1978: Panchen Lama released.
1979: First exile delegation visits Tibet; Deng Xiaoping–Gyalo Thondup contacts begin.
1980: Hu Yaobang visits Tibet.
1987: Dalai Lama's Five Point Peace Plan; protests in Lhasa.
1988: Strasbourg Proposal.
1987–1989: Series of Lhasa protests.
1989: Death of the 10th Panchen Lama; martial law declared in Lhasa; Dalai Lama awarded Nobel Peace Prize.
1995: Dalai Lama recognizes Gedhun Choekyi Nyima as 11th Panchen Lama; his detention; China installs Gyaincain Norbu.
2000: 17th Karmapa Ogyen Trinley Dorje escapes to India.
2002: Resumption of Sino-Tibetan dialogue.
2006: Qinghai–Tibet railway opens.
2008: March protests and riots in Lhasa and across Tibetan areas; talks continue.
2009: Self-immolations begin (Tapey).
2011: Dalai Lama devolves political authority; Lobsang Sangay elected Sikyong.
2012: Peak of self-immolation protests.
2020: Tibetan Policy and Support Act (US).
2021: Penpa Tsering elected Sikyong; Xi Jinping visits Tibet.
2025: Dalai Lama's 90th birthday; statement on continuation of the Dalai Lama institution and Gaden Phodrang Trust authority over reincarnation.
